name: notebooklm-mcp
description: Automate Google NotebookLM at scale — citation-backed Q&A, Studio generation, multi-account rotation.
long_description: MCP server for Google NotebookLM. Citation-backed Q&A from your notebooks, full Studio generation (audio overview, video, infographic, report, presentation, data table), source and notebook management, multi-account rotation with auto-reauth, and batch_to_vault for offline answer caching. Also ships a local HTTP REST API for use from n8n, Zapier, Make and scripts. Runs locally — it drives a real browser session against your own Google account.

data_dir.title: Data directory
data_dir.description: Where NotebookLM MCP stores its Chrome profile and session data. Leave blank to use the default location.

tools.name: notebook_ask
tools.description: Ask a question against a notebook's sources and get a citation-backed answer. Session-based — follow-ups keep prior context.

session_id.description: Optional session ID for contextual conversations. If omitted, a new session is created.

notebook_id.description: Optional notebook ID from your library. If omitted, uses the active notebook. Use list_notebooks to see available notebooks.

notebook_url.description: Optional notebook URL (overrides notebook_id). Use this for ad-hoc queries to notebooks not in your library.

show_browser.description: Show browser window for debugging (simple version). For advanced control (typing speed, stealth, etc.), use browser_options instead.

source_format.description:
Format for source citation extraction (default: none). Options:
- none: No source extraction (fastest)
- inline: Insert source text inline: "text [1: source excerpt]"
- footnotes: Append sources at the end as footnotes
- json: Return sources as separate object in response
- expanded: Replace [1] with full quoted source text

Note: Source extraction adds ~1-2 seconds but does NOT consume additional NotebookLM quota.

browser_options.description: Optional browser behavior settings. Claude can control everything: visibility, typing speed, stealth mode, timeouts. Useful for debugging or fine-tuning.

show.description: Show browser window (default: from ENV or false)

headless.description: Run browser in headless mode (default: true)

stealth.description: Human-like behavior settings to avoid detection

enabled.description: Master switch for all stealth features (default: true)

delay_min_ms.description: Minimum delay between actions in ms (default: 100)

delay_max_ms.description: Maximum delay between actions in ms (default: 400)

data.description: The tool payload on success. The exact shape depends on the tool.

error.description: Human-readable error message, present only when success is false.

show_browser.description: Show browser window during creation. Default: false (headless).

show_browser.description: Show browser window during deletion. Default: false (headless).

tools.name: server_cleanup
tools.description: Deep-scan and clean up all NotebookLM MCP data files across the system.

confirm.description: Confirmation flag. Tool shows preview first, then user confirms deletion. Set to true only after user has reviewed the preview and explicitly confirmed.

preserve_library.description: Preserve library.json file during cleanup. Default: false. Set to true to keep your notebook library while deleting everything else (browser data, caches, logs).

tools.name: vault_batch
tools.description: Run a list of questions against a notebook and persist each answer to disk as markdown + JSON artifacts.

questions.description: Non-empty array of question strings to ask sequentially.

vault_dir.description: Destination directory (absolute or relative). Created with mkdir -p if missing.

notebook_id.description: Optional library notebook id to query. Falls back to the active notebook.

notebook_url.description: Optional NotebookLM URL (overrides notebook_id). Use for ad-hoc notebooks.

source_format.description: Citation extraction mode. "json" (default) preserves titles + excerpts in the sidecar.

sleep_between_ms.description: Pause between questions in ms. 1500–3000 is sane for batches above ~20.

session_id.description: Optional session id to reuse for context continuity across the batch.

data.description: The tool payload on success. The exact shape depends on the tool.

error.description: Human-readable error message, present only when success is false.
